about.bio: Unity game developer with 2+ years of hands-on experience shipping 2D and 3D titles across Android and PC. I focus on gameplay programming, performance optimization, and modular systems that scale from prototype to live build.

skills.name: Unity Engine & C#
skills.description: Gameplay programming, game loops, player controllers, camera systems, and physics-driven mechanics.

skills.name: Performance Optimization
skills.description: Profiling, object pooling, runtime culling, and script refactors for stable framerate targets.

skills.name: Gameplay Systems
skills.description: State machines, event-driven architecture, inventory systems, and procedural gameplay logic.

skills.name: AR/VR Simulation
skills.description: AR Foundation workflows and interaction features implemented during Unity internship projects.

skills.name: Tools & Pipelines
skills.description: Git, Visual Studio, basic Blender and Photoshop integration for production-ready iteration.

skills.name: Cross-Engine Development
skills.description: Godot experimentation and game-jam development alongside Unity production workflows.

projects.name: The Keyhole
projects.description: First-person horror project in development with inventory mechanics, event-driven scare logic, and procedural room generation.
projects.role: Project Lead, Lead Programmer, and Game Design Lead
projects.whatILearned: Evolved a procedural horror concept with context-aware anomaly systems and branching narrative consequences.

concepts: Procedural rooms blended with handcrafted high-stakes moments.
Context-aware anomaly generation based on behavior and room state.
Narrative replay paths driven by moral choices and memory fragments.
mechanics: Exploration loop around keys, locked doors, and secret routes.
Anomalies that alter perception, movement, and environmental trust signals.
Sound/light/stealth triad as survival toolkit.
lessons: Behavior-reactive horror systems increase replayability and tension depth.

responsibilities: Directed project from prototype phase to demo phase and launch track.
Led core programming architecture and feature execution.
Led game design direction and flow decisions.
Supported art and music production/integration.

projects.name: Fasteroid
projects.description: Mobile arcade game shipped on Google Play with progression systems, player controls, and monetization-ready architecture.
projects.role: Lead Programmer, Co-Director, and Game Design Lead
projects.whatILearned: Managed live mobile launch pipeline from monetization to services integration and validated retention with immediate user traction.

concepts: Mobile ads integration flow.
Google Play services integration and leaderboard setup.
Production coordination for live release.
mechanics: Tap-and-steer meteor control with speed-based scaling challenge.
Unlockable cosmetics and multiple visual sky themes.
lessons: Launch readiness requires both technical and team-management discipline.
Monetization/integration quality impacts post-release stability.
results: Crossed 1,000 downloads in less than 24 hours.

responsibilities: Led primary programming and systems integration.
Co-directed project execution and release priorities.
Led core game design and mechanic shaping for launch build.

projects.name: Sky Racer
projects.description: A thrilling game where you navigate a car through 3D space with narrow roads. Tap to change direction as the road shifts — a test of quick reflexes and precise timing!
projects.whatILearned: Built a responsive ZigZag-inspired 3D runner with tighter controls, escalating difficulty, and procedural progression while handling full gameplay/audio loop solo.

concepts: 3D camera follow and smooth player tracking in Unity.
Local high score storage with PlayerPrefs.
Audio and SFX orchestration for gameplay events.
Infinite platform generation for endless gameplay.
3D model and animation integration.
challenges: Designing stable infinite platform generation logic.
Working with 3D model pipeline and asset setup.
Tuning Unity particle system behavior for feedback.
mechanics: Improved tap responsiveness compared to reference game feel.
Auto-changing backgrounds over time.
Speed scaling as score increases.
Animated diamond collectibles.
Difficulty ramp through terrain complexity.

responsibilities: Programmed all gameplay systems end-to-end.
Designed the full game loop, progression, and balancing.
Handled implementation and iteration as a solo creator.

projects.name: Fast Ball
projects.description: A minimalistic hypercasual game where the score increases as you touch platforms and you die if you collide with enemies. Features main menu, scene management, and audio options.
projects.whatILearned: Improved minimal hypercasual game feel through controlled spawning, particle-driven feedback, and cleaner scene/UI flow.

concepts: Particle invocation on object destruction.
Text animation workflows in Unity.
Scene management for core game flow.
challenges: Building good-feeling particle effects.
Balancing enemy spawn intervals to preserve player experience.
mechanics: Main menu flow with scene transitions.
Audio mute/disable option.
Back to menu and quit controls.

responsibilities: Built gameplay and state flow as sole programmer.
Designed the core hypercasual mechanics and pacing.
Owned full implementation and polish decisions.

projects.name: TheFishMaster
projects.description: A fishing game where the hook is cast for a certain length and catches fishes as it comes back. Features dragging mechanics, fish spawning at different water levels, and idle earning.
projects.whatILearned: Implemented touch dragging, depth-based fish spawning, and idle-style progression in a hook-return fishing loop.

concepts: Mobile drag interaction for hook control.
Scroll-like hook movement and return timing.
Fish spawning across multiple water depths.
Idle earning systems.
challenges: Making drag behavior feel consistent on mobile.
Balancing spawn behavior by depth.
Implementing idle progression with Unity systems.

responsibilities: Implemented full gameplay code and core systems solo.
Designed the fishing loop and progression logic.
Owned tuning and iteration of mechanics and feel.

projects.name: Collect It!
projects.description: Collect blobs to refill your energy bar before it depletes! Features touch-to-move, random blob spawning, and player bounce mechanics.
projects.whatILearned: Polished a clean energy-loop prototype with responsive touch movement, spawn pacing, and boundary bounce behavior.

concepts: Touch-to-move control loop.
Random collectible spawning logic.
Energy depletion over time as survival pressure.
Screen restrictions and bounce response.
challenges: Finalizing bounce behavior against boundaries.
Maintaining control clarity while reusing assets.

responsibilities: Programmed full game loop and controls as sole developer.
Designed mechanics, fail conditions, and balancing.
Managed technical implementation and polish solo.

projects.name: Shinobi Runner
projects.description: A Naruto fan game with split-screen gameplay. Two characters dodge kunai knives — tap each side of the screen to dodge. If one dies, game over!
projects.whatILearned: Shipped a split-screen mobile dodge prototype with dual touch zones, synchronized hazards, and adaptive layout.

concepts: Per-side touch detection for two simultaneous players.
Infinite kunai spawn loop.
Vector sprite handling in Unity.
Responsive multi-device mobile layout.
challenges: Dual-player touch control reliability.
Spawn distance and randomization tuning.
Collider setup for two active player lanes.

projects.name: Shoot It!
projects.description: A basketball game where you drag and shoot the ball into the post. Miss 3 shots and you're out! Features basketball physics, lives system, and 2D vector art.
projects.whatILearned: Learned sports-arcade tuning by combining drag-shoot input, basketball physics, and life-based fail conditions.

concepts: Drag-and-shoot input implementation.
Basketball-style trajectory/physics tuning.
Impact audio for court and contact events.
Lives system with clear UI state.
2D vector art pipeline handling.
challenges: Understanding and tuning basketball physics.
Stabilizing drag-shoot trajectory behavior.
Managing 2D vector art integration.
lessons: Identified future scope for multiplayer and additional modes after prototype completion.

projects.name: Donkey Kong Clone
projects.description: A recreation of the classic Donkey Kong game from Gameboy times! Features vertical climbing, barrel physics, level progression, pixel art, and animations.
projects.whatILearned: Recreated classic Donkey Kong-style gameplay under tight time constraints and strengthened retro-system implementation skills.

concepts: Vertical climb flow and ladder traversal.
Barrel movement/interaction physics.
Level transitions in Unity.
Pixel-art workflow and frame timing.
Animation with frame-skip control.
challenges: Reverse-engineering original game behavior.
Tuning barrel physics to feel authentic.
Managing pixel art and frame pacing constraints.

responsibilities: Recreated gameplay systems and level flow in Unity.
Implemented controls, hazards, and progression solo.
Handled retro design adaptation and implementation decisions.

projects.name: Hotshot
projects.description: A game made for Polymars x ScoreSpace Game Jam in 3 days. Built with a team of 4 across timezones. Features a leaderboard system and was submitted with just 10 minutes left!
projects.whatILearned: Delivered a team jam build under timezone constraints, implemented leaderboard work, and shipped minutes before deadline.

lessons: Cross-timezone planning requires explicit daily coordination.
Clear role ownership improves jam velocity.
Problem-solving under deadline pressure is a core production skill.
challenges: Lost first day due to timezone overlap issues.
Leaderboard implementation complexity late in production.
Last-minute stabilization and submission with 10 minutes left.
results: Submitted successfully in final 10 minutes after 3-day jam sprint.

responsibilities: Led team execution and production flow during jam timeline.
Owned core programming decisions and primary gameplay implementation.
Integrated online leaderboard systems.
Contributed to art tasks where needed.

projects.name: Squishy Sophie
projects.description: A puzzle platformer for GMTK Jam 2024. Play as Sophie the cat on a mission to reach the milk! Dodge dogs, eat fish to grow, avoid yarn balls. Theme: 'Built to Scale'.
projects.role: Team lead — programming, team coordination, and art
projects.whatILearned: Handled team leadership, programming, and art trade-offs while cutting risky scope to ship a stable jam entry.

lessons: Frequent progress checks are essential when leading larger jam teams.
If a feature exceeds feasible time budget, cut or redesign quickly.
Aseprite tooling shortcuts dramatically improve asset turnaround.
challenges: Scrapped squeeze/stretch mechanic due to bugs and time cost.
Removed four levels after a critical bug to preserve overall stability.
Submission issues resolved in final minutes.
mechanics: Scale-driven cat progression loop (growth/shrink risk-reward).
Hazard/collectible interaction balancing under scope pressure.

projects.name: Crashed Ship
projects.description: A space exploration game for the Mini Jam (4 days). Theme: 'Earth' with 'no green' constraint. Find scattered spaceship parts and repair your ship to return home!
projects.whatILearned: Built a complete jam loop under a strict visual constraint and improved prioritization, communication, and rapid iteration habits.

concepts: Player navigation and interaction loop in exploration context.
Collectible parts system tied to repair objective.
Constraint-driven visual direction for theme adaptation.
challenges: Delivering full loop within 4-day schedule.
Balancing scope across mechanics, art, and polish.
lessons: Constraints can unlock stronger creative direction.
Knowing when to move on is critical in short jams.
Team communication quality directly affects execution speed.

responsibilities: Directed team flow and milestone planning.
Led gameplay programming and technical integration.
Contributed to pixel art production.
Contributed to music/audio support for the project.

projects.name: Joe the Scammer
projects.description: A quirky game about a shark running a 'fishy' pawn shop, overpricing junk to sea creatures. Made for IndieDevGameJam5. Secured 7th place!
projects.whatILearned: Improved branch-based collaboration and art/style consistency while shipping a polished comedic economy loop.

concepts: Dialogue-driven pawn-shop gameplay loop.
Economy-flavored interaction design with humor framing.
lessons: GitHub branch workflow reduced merge friction in team jam.
Maintaining style consistency is harder with reduced art bandwidth.
Structured task coordination kept team aligned to deadline.
results: Uploaded 3 hours before deadline and secured 7th place.

responsibilities: Led team execution and delivery strategy.
Handled primary gameplay/programming responsibilities.
Supported pixel art work where needed.
Contributed to music/audio implementation support.

projects.name: Bubble Chef
projects.description: Global Game Jam 2025 entry. Play as a bubble-shaped chef catching ingredients to create Indian dishes like vada pav and pani puri! 3D visuals with chaotic cooking action.
projects.whatILearned: Shipped a hybrid remote/on-site jam project and strengthened high-pressure collaboration, task triage, and mechanic debugging.

mechanics: Bubble-based ingredient catch loop with recipe assembly pressure.
Penalty conditions for wrong ingredient catches and bubble loss.
lessons: Hybrid team setup can work with disciplined communication.
Tight task/resource planning is mandatory in short jams.
Rapid debug/iteration cycles improve final game feel.

projects.name: Redacted Skies
projects.description: A retro-inspired adventure about survival, speed, and determination. 3-day jam with 200+ entries. Placed 41st overall and 16th for best theme use!
projects.whatILearned: Coordinated rapid build cadence across design, code, and polish to deliver a ranked retro-action jam project.

concepts: Story-first framing for jam scope alignment.
High-speed evasion mechanics (fighter jets and homing threats).
lessons: Focused collaboration under short windows compounds quality.
Final-day bug fixing and polish often define ranking outcomes.
results: Placed 41st overall and 16th for best theme use out of 200+ entries.

projects.name: Knight Spade
projects.description: A card-based game for the Love Game Jam. Pick a card to get a random power-up and defeat the boss! 12th in graphics, 41st overall out of 400+ entries.
projects.role: Team Lead and Lead Lua Programmer
projects.whatILearned: Expanded beyond Unity comfort zone, built in Love2D/Lua, and designed replayable card-driven combat progression.

mechanics: Random card selection at run start determines power-up path.
Boss progression driven by card-selected build variance.
lessons: Switching frameworks opens new problem-solving approaches.
Community-driven jams accelerate adaptation to unfamiliar stacks.
results: Placed 12th in graphics and 41st overall among 400+ entries.

responsibilities: Led overall game flow and team coordination.
Owned primary Lua/Love2D programming tasks.
Co-led game design direction and gameplay decisions.
Contributed to music/audio support.

projects.name: Glitch in the Grid
projects.description: A fast-paced bullet-hell shooter for BulletHellJam. Grid-based combat with unique power-ups, dynamic enemy waves, and healing orbs. 30th out of 300+ entries!
projects.role: Team Lead and Lead Lua Programmer
projects.whatILearned: Built and polished a competitive bullet-hell loop in 5 focused days, proving execution quality under compressed timelines.

mechanics: Grid-based fast combat with escalating bullet patterns.
Power-up system: Guardian Shield, Nuke, Rapid Fire.
Dynamic wave scaling and healing-orb clutch recoveries.
lessons: Focused scope and polish can outperform longer timelines.
Tight controls and clarity are core to bullet-hell satisfaction.
results: Placed 30th out of 300+ entries in BulletHellJam.

responsibilities: Led production flow and task coordination across team.
Owned core Lua/Love2D gameplay programming.
Contributed to game design and pacing decisions.
Contributed to music/audio support.

projects.name: Amnesia
projects.description: A 3D horror game for a week-long jam. Wake up in an unfamiliar house, find clues, identify the impostor among faceless silhouettes. 90th out of 500+ entries!
projects.role: Lead Godot (GDScript) Programmer and Game Design Contributor
projects.whatILearned: Implemented a clue-driven psychological horror loop in Godot with branching outcomes and high-pressure production discipline.

mechanics: Timed clue collection phase followed by impostor-guess resolution.
Dialogue contradiction detection tied to discovered clues.
Multiple unsettling endings based on player choice.
lessons: Atmosphere and audio design are critical force multipliers in horror.
Strong core loop design matters more than raw content volume in jam scope.
results: Placed 90th out of 500+ entries (70th in music, 21 positive ratings).

responsibilities: Led Godot/GDScript gameplay implementation.
Played a major role in core game design direction.
Drove technical decisions for horror loop execution.

projects.name: Deep Bound
projects.description: Made for GMTK Game Jam. An underwater survival game where dying doesn't end the run — it changes you. Built in 1.5 days, submitted 3 minutes before deadline!
projects.whatILearned: Shipped a high-concept role-reversal survival prototype after starting late, while handling merge conflicts and deadline risk.

mechanics: Death changes role/state instead of ending run.
Human-vs-spirit phase flow with timed intermission and underwater pressure loop.
challenges: Started development at end of day 2 with ~1.5 days left.
Resolved GitHub merge conflicts near submission window.
lessons: Rapid prototyping and role-swapping accelerate jam delivery.
Shipping under pressure requires aggressive decision-making.

responsibilities: Led team delivery under extreme jam constraints.
Owned primary programming responsibilities.
Contributed the largest share of game design decisions.
Directed implementation priorities while managing team flow.

projects.name: Stardew Valley Clone
projects.description: A farming-based game inspired by Stardew Valley, built while learning Godot. Exploring farming mechanics, character interactions, and charming world-building.
projects.role: Solo developer — learning Godot through recreation
projects.whatILearned: Used this project to onboard into Godot: tilemaps, GDScript workflow, and early farming/interaction systems.

concepts: Godot tile sets and tilemaps for reusable world building.
GDScript fundamentals and Python-like scripting flow.
Farming and interaction loop prototyping.
lessons: Engine migration improves design perspective and tool adaptability.

projects.name: Making a Game in Every Language
projects.description: Building a playable game in every popular programming language — Python, C#, C++, Java, JS, TS, Lua, Rust, Go, Dart, Kotlin, Swift, Ruby and more!
projects.whatILearned: Designed a long-horizon polyglot learning system to compare gameplay architecture across languages, engines, and paradigms.

concepts: Cross-language game-loop implementation patterns.
Framework trade-offs across Python, Unity/C#, C++, Java, JS/TS, Lua, Rust, Go, Dart, Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby ecosystems.
lessons: Language diversity strengthens architecture thinking and portability decisions.
Reusable core mechanics expose engine-specific strengths/limitations quickly.

projects.name: Terrific Traffic
projects.description: Upcoming hypercasual arcade project where you play as a chaos manager at a 4-way intersection and strategically delete traffic while preserving reputation.
projects.role: Team Lead, Lead Programmer, and Game Design Lead
projects.whatILearned: Prototype-first development helped validate a novel traffic-chaos mechanic where every destructive action is balanced by a reputation economy.

concepts: Risk-reward loop design around reputation cost.
Intersection pressure modeling in hypercasual pacing.
mechanics: Tap-to-delete traffic as primary interaction model.
Reputation as fail-state pressure instead of traditional health/lives.
lessons: A strong one-line hook can drive clearer prototype decisions early.

responsibilities: Leading team execution and production direction.
Owning primary programming and systems implementation.
Leading game design and flow decisions from prototype onward.

blogs.title: Game Design with Game Programming Patterns
blogs.description: A deep dive into Game Programming Patterns by Robert Nystrom — covering Command, Observer, State, Component, Game Loop, and Flyweight patterns for building robust game systems.

blogs.title: Making a Game in Every Language — Day 1
blogs.description: Launching my dream project: building a playable game in every popular programming language. Day 1: Space Shooter in Python with Pygame!

achievements.title: 12th in Graphics — Love Game Jam (Knight Spade)
30th out of 300+ — BulletHellJam (Glitch in the Grid)

alchemist: Welcome, adventurer! I'm the Skill Alchemist.
Each crystal here represents a skill Saurabh has mastered.
Press E near the crystals to learn more!
miner: Yo! I guard the Project Mines.
Every chest contains a project Saurabh has built.
Some are game jam entries, some are passion projects!
shaman: Greetings, young one. I keep the Hall of Achievements.
Each statue tells the story of a milestone.
Saurabh's journey has been quite remarkable.
villageLady: He's a game developer from Mumbai.
Check out the terminal at the end to get in touch!

resumeHighlights.value: Improved mid-range builds from ~45 FPS to stable 60 FPS

resumeHighlights.value: Fasteroid reached 1,000+ active users on day one

resumeHighlights.label: Studio Role
resumeHighlights.value: Founder & Lead Game Developer at Stopwatch Games

resumes.label: Unity Developer Resume
resumes.focus: Unity gameplay engineering, optimization, and AR/VR simulation work

resumes.label: Game Developer Resume
resumes.focus: General game development profile for multi-platform production

bullets: Designed and shipped Unity projects spanning core gameplay, player mechanics, level logic, and UI.
Built modular C# architecture for fast iteration, testing, and post-release feature expansion.
Managed live mobile and PC builds with performance tuning, bug fixing, and release maintenance.
Integrated AR/VR simulation features in Unity with focus on interaction quality and runtime stability.
Optimized gameplay systems and scenes; improved average performance from ~45 FPS to stable 60 FPS on mid-range devices.
Collaborated with designers and artists to debug issues and refine in-game interaction loops.

hireMe.heading: Hire Me For
hireMe.availability: Available for freelance, contract, and collaborative game projects. Open to part-time and full-time discussions.
hireMe.mode: Remote-first (India timezone), flexible for async global teams.
preferredProjects: Gameplay systems and core loop design
Mobile production and Play Store shipping
Game jam and rapid prototype execution

services.title: Gameplay Systems
services.details: Player controllers, state machines, combat/interaction loops, camera systems, and reusable core mechanics.

services.title: Optimization & Profiling
services.details: FPS stabilization, memory/performance tuning, object pooling, runtime culling, and script refactoring.

services.title: Mobile Shipping
services.details: Production-ready Android builds, store preparation, Play Services integration, and post-release iteration.

services.title: Technical Leadership
services.details: Team-leading implementation flow, feature prioritization, architecture decisions, and milestone execution.

careerTimeline.title: Python (Pygame) Developer
careerTimeline.summary: Started with Python fundamentals and transitioned into 2D game development using Pygame, building early playable projects and core programming confidence.
highlights: Built foundational gameplay loops and input systems in Pygame.
Shipped early practice projects while learning rapid iteration habits.

careerTimeline.title: Android Game Development
careerTimeline.summary: Focused on Unity-based Android development and hypercasual design cycles, building multiple games quickly to sharpen production speed.
highlights: Built 7 hypercasual games in a 7-day challenge.
Strengthened mobile control tuning and gameplay balancing workflows.

careerTimeline.title: Unity Developer Intern
careerTimeline.summary: Contributed as a Unity developer intern, supporting gameplay and simulation features while collaborating in a production team environment.
highlights: Worked directly with team leadership on implementation tasks.
Improved cross-functional collaboration with design and product stakeholders.

careerTimeline.title: Founder
careerTimeline.summary: Founded and lead Stopwatch Games, driving project direction from concept to launch with a focus on gameplay quality and shipping discipline.
highlights: Leading technical implementation and game design direction.
Managing production flow from prototype to release-ready builds.

testimonials.name: Santhosh Raghavendran Rao
testimonials.role: Business Development Manager at Nexora Neo Solutions
testimonials.quote: Saurabh quickly picked up new technologies, delivered clean and efficient code, and contributed across both front-end and back-end tasks with reliability and strong teamwork.

testimonials.name: Abderrahman El Qadiri
testimonials.relationship: Worked with Saurabh on the same team
testimonials.quote: Saurabh brings a rare blend of technical insight, creative vision, and leadership maturity. He consistently drives momentum through complex development cycles and team pressure.

testimonials.name: Lineet Kayani
testimonials.relationship: Worked with Saurabh on the same team
testimonials.quote: Saurabh followed direction effectively and helped transform team ideas into a well-built website/landing page, showing dependable execution and ownership in internship work.
